danger_signals.visual_form: Decreasing energy bar display during fight
danger_signals.meaning: Boxer is accumulating damage and approaching knockdown threshold

danger_signals.visual_form: Separate head and body damage indicators reaching zero
danger_signals.meaning: Localized vulnerability — continued hits to damaged area trigger TKO

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual scarring/wound sprites on boxer's head
danger_signals.meaning: Severe head damage sustained; boxer approaching critical condition
Third knockdown ends fight immediately; progressive danger escalation

danger_signals.visual_form: Character animation showing defensive or unbalanced stance
danger_signals.meaning: Boxer is weakened and vulnerable to finishing combinations

safety_signals.visual_form: Complete/near-complete energy bar at start of round or after rest
safety_signals.meaning: Boxer is fresh and at peak defensive capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Head and body meters showing minimal or no damage
safety_signals.meaning: No localized vulnerability; boxer can absorb distributed damage evenly

safety_signals.visual_form: No knockdowns registered at start of fight or after recovery
safety_signals.meaning: Boxer has margin for safety; can absorb one-two more knockdowns
Boxer is in offensive position and maintaining control

faction_indicators.color_identity: Selectable from: red, green, gold, purple, dark blue, cyan, yellow, or silver (The Beast)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinguishable by custom coloring vs. preset opponent color schemes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ring position displayed on overhead tactical map; sprite renders from side-on camera view

faction_indicators.color_identity: Preset color schemes per fighter (contextually distinct from player color)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each opponent has distinct name, nation attribution, and sprite variation; ranking system (Champion at rank 1 down to rank 28) creates visual hierarchy
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Career mode progression displays opponent rank and nation; overhead map shows opponent position and movement relative to player

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Title holder status indicated by ranking position (Rank 1 / Champion)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Holyfield has maximum attributes (power, speed, stamina, defense) compared to all other boxers; sprite reflects champion tier
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final boss encounter in career mode; defeats Holyfield results in title acquisition and transition to 'challenge matches' phase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lower-ranked opponents appear in career ladder; player boxer sprite shows user-selected appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents have lower AI difficulty; attribute gains through training visible in progression system

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent rank numbers decrease (higher position in ladder); rank displays change as boxer ascends
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent difficulty increases; training effects become more noticeable in stat distribution

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent is Holyfield with maximum-tier sprite and styling; World Heavyweight Title on display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Holyfield behaves with maximum attributes (speed, power, stamina, defense); near-unbeatable baseline opponent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title holder status reflected in UI; challenge match opponents have higher visual distinctiveness
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High-ranked contenders appear with elevated AI difficulty; player boxer subject to age/fatigue degradation after 20-25 fights

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent roster gradually replaces aging boxers with new ones; ranking structure shifts dynamically
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player boxer attributes decline; training becomes less effective; mandatory retirement triggered at 40 fights

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Holyfield's ranking drops visibly in career progression display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Holyfield declines in rankings and eventually retires; only resetting internal game memory restores him to optimum form

environmental_storytelling.example: Ring navigation displayed from above showing both fighters' positions; allows 360-degree spatial awareness and communicates ring control/positioning without explicit UI text
Separate head and body meters communicate targeted injury; visible cuts on head sprite show cumulative damage story without text narration
Numbered opponent roster (1-28) visually represents climb through boxing hierarchy; ascending numbers-to-names progression tells career narrative
Boxer appearance reflects damage state (stagger, cuts, bruising); posture shifts communicate story of punishment without dialogue
Training effects plateau and reverse after 20-25 fights, visually communicating aging boxer decline without explicit narrative
Holyfield maximum attributes and final-boss positioning in career ladder communicate title significance through game systems rather than storytelling

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responsive controls, full energy meter, capable of landing powerful combinations
Takes amplified damage to that specific area; continued hits to damaged zone trigger TKO progression
Recovery animation plays; boxer returns to ring but knockdown counter increments; behavioral vulnerability increases
Fight ends immediately; no recovery possible; boxing match decision rendered
Elevated attribute values; faster, more powerful, better stamina and defense AI routines
Lower attribute values; slower AI movement, weaker punch impact, reduced defense patterns
Maximum attributes across all four categories (power, speed, stamina, defense); behaves as final-boss-tier opponent
Ranking declines; eventually exits roster and retires; no longer appears in career progression unless game memory reset
Digitized audio 'Break' command triggers; referee animation separates boxers; communicates round pacing and clinch rules
Computer boxer triggers pause and delivers digitized speech insult ('come on and fight you wimp!'); communicates personality/confidence state

notes: Evander Holyfield's Real Deal Boxing is a sports simulation with minimalist narrative design. World-state and danger communication rely entirely on mechanical systems (meters, counters, attributes) and sprite-based visual feedback rather than explicit HUD text or dialogue. The game communicates political hierarchy (boxer rankings, champion tier) through progression structure and attribute distribution. Faction identity is minimal — the game distinguishes player vs. opponent through color selection and sprite rendering, with no true faction systems. Environmental storytelling operates through damage visualization (cuts, stagger) and ranking dynamics (opponent progression, champion decline). The game's core manifestation layer communicates state change through energy depletion, damage localization, knockdown counting, and attribute degradation over career time.
